gap_analysis.description: Unity Catalog uses an additive (allowlist) permission model with no DENY mechanism.
gap_analysis.remediation: Restructure into separate schemas with restricted grants. Use row filters for partial access. Remove principals from over-privileged groups.

gap_analysis.description: Ranger delegateAdmin allows principals to manage policies for their resources. Unity Catalog uses MANAGE privilege instead.
gap_analysis.remediation: Grant MANAGE privilege on the corresponding UC object, or assign the principal as a metastore/catalog admin if broader admin access is needed.

gap_analysis.description: Ranger wildcard table policies (database.*) translate to schema-level grants in UC. Verify this is intentional.
gap_analysis.remediation: Review if schema-level grants are appropriate. Consider granting on specific tables for least-privilege access.

gap_analysis.description: These policies use isDenyAllElse=true in Ranger, meaning all principals not explicitly listed are denied. Unity Catalog has no equivalent — generated grants will not restrict unlisted principals.
gap_analysis.remediation: Audit all principals with access to these resources in UC. Only grant access to explicitly listed principals.

gap_analysis.description: These policies have validity schedules (start/end times) in Ranger. Unity Catalog grants are permanent — there is no built-in expiry mechanism.

items.detail: Policy "db=hr*;udf=udf" is time-limited in Ranger (2018/01/12 14:32:00 → 2020/02/13 12:16:00) — UC grants are permanent

gap_analysis.remediation: Manage grant lifecycle manually or via automation. Revoke grants in UC when the validity period ends.

gap_analysis.description: These policies include conditions (e.g. ip-range, time-of-day, custom evaluators) that restrict access in Ranger but have no UC equivalent. Generated grants apply unconditionally.

items.detail: Policy "db=finance; table=fin_*; column=salary" has conditions [ip-range] with no UC equivalent — grants will apply unconditionally

gap_analysis.remediation: Enforce IP-range and context-based restrictions at the network or application layer. Review each affected policy carefully before executing the migration SQL.

warnings.message: Policy "db=hr*;udf=udf" is time-limited in Ranger (2018/01/12 14:32:00 → 2020/02/13 12:16:00) — UC grants are permanent
warnings.recommendation: Manage the grant lifecycle manually or via automation. Revoke the grant in UC when the validity period ends.

warnings.message: DENY policy for group3 on default.test2 [SELECT, CREATE] — Unity Catalog has no DENY mechanism
warnings.recommendation: Restructure: move sensitive data into a separate schema with restricted grants, or use row filters/column masks to limit visibility instead of denying access.
